assertionResults.title: Architecture storytelling surfaces the required BETTER stack layers
assertionResults.reason: The architecture section explicitly names Hyperliquid/HyperEVM, OpenServ/BRAID, proprietary AI/RL models, Polygon node/validator operations, and low-latency/colo/FPGA execution, with plain-language role copy for each layer.

steps.action: Open the local app and navigate to #architecture
steps.expected: The architecture section is visible with stack-layer storytelling
steps.observed: Technical Architecture loaded with BETTER Stack Architecture content and layer cards

steps.action: Review the stack-layer cards and section copy
steps.expected: All required BETTER layers and their plain-language roles are surfaced
steps.observed: The section copy and cards described Hyperliquid/HyperEVM, OpenServ/BRAID, AI/RL, Polygon node/validator ops, and low-latency/colo/FPGA roles

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR requests were triggered during this read-only section review.

assertionResults.title: Architecture layers show maturity and dependency sequencing
assertionResults.reason: Layer cards visibly show Live, In Progress, Planned, and Speculative badges, and later-stage layers include explicit dependency text such as Polygon depending on Hyperliquid/HyperEVM and low-latency execution depending on earlier infrastructure.

steps.expected: Users can distinguish layer maturity states
steps.observed: Cards showed visible status badges including In Progress, Live, Planned, and Speculative

steps.action: Check later-stage layer cards for dependency cues
steps.observed: Polygon Node & Validator Operations listed 'Depends on: Hyperliquid / HyperEVM' and Low-Latency / Colo / FPGA Execution listed both Polygon and Hyperliquid dependencies

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR requests were triggered while inspecting architecture status/dependency copy.

assertionResults.title: Cost bands show assumption hooks and capability unlocks
assertionResults.reason: Expanded cost-band phases use rough ranges instead of exact budgets and each phase exposes an assumption basis, capability unlock, and dependency hook where applicable.

steps.expected: Subsystems reveal phased ranges for each stage
steps.observed: All five subsystem accordions expanded and showed multi-phase cost ranges

steps.expected: Each phase shows estimate framing and what the spend unlocks
steps.observed: Phases displayed Cost Range, Assumption Basis, Capability Unlock, and readable Requires dependencies

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR requests were triggered during cost-band exploration.

assertionResults.title: Heavy visuals degrade gracefully under constrained conditions
assertionResults.reason: Under iPhone 14 emulation with reduced motion enabled, the hero and architecture sections remained readable, navigation to #architecture still worked, and architecture interactions remained usable without console errors.

steps.action: Emulate a constrained/mobile environment (iPhone 14 + reduced motion) and load the page
steps.expected: The site preserves readable copy and usable controls
steps.observed: The hero remained readable with visible menu structure and no broken overlays

steps.action: Navigate to the architecture section and interact with architecture controls
steps.expected: Architecture content stays usable on constrained/mobile conditions
steps.observed: The architecture heading and first layer card remained legible, accordion toggles remained tappable, and interaction recording completed without runtime errors

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR requests were triggered during constrained/mobile validation.

assertionResults.title: Infrastructure evolution and BETTER flywheel storytelling are explorable
assertionResults.reason: The flywheel area renders as an explorable grid of infrastructure, product, revenue, and token nodes, and selecting nodes opens a detail panel with maturity, explanatory copy, and feed relationships.

steps.action: Scroll to the BETTER Flywheel section
steps.expected: An explorable flywheel surface is present
steps.observed: The page showed categorized infrastructure/product/revenue/token nodes under 'The BETTER Flywheel'

steps.action: Click flywheel nodes and review the detail state
steps.expected: Users can explore node details and relationships
steps.observed: Clicking nodes such as Social Vaults & vBETTER and Autonomous Strategy Agents opened a detail panel with maturity, explanatory copy, and feeds-into/fed-by relationships

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR requests were triggered during flywheel exploration.

summary: Tested 5 assigned architecture/flywheel assertions in a single agent-browser session: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. The architecture section, cost-band explorer, and flywheel surface were all present and usable, including under mobile/reduced-motion emulation.
